title: Hoom

{{Listen3|Hoom-PhlogLanguage-Ewoks1-3.flac|Hoom speaking the Phlog language|Hoom speaks to Nahkee in the Phlog language}}
'''Hoom''' was a [[Phlog/Legends|Phlog]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], his [[Youngling/Legends|little]] brother [[Nahkee]] was [[First Phlog crisis|kidnapped]]. He and his [[Parent/Legends|parents]], [[Zut]] and [[Dobah]], found evidence—planted by the [[Witch/Legends|witch]] [[Morag]]—of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] involvement. They thundered toward the Ewoks' [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], running into several other Ewoks along the way, all of whom they shouted at and scared.

When they reached the village, the Ewok warriors' defense was no match for the towering Phlogs. However, before they began their butchery, several [[Wokling/Legends|woklings]] brought Nahkee back, having recovered him from the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] with whom Morag had placed the baby. Hoom and his family were delighted to have Nahkee back—they scared away the Duloks, who had followed the Ewoks, and made peace with Bright Tree Village.

==Biography==
===To Hoom it may concern===
{{Quote|Hoom!|Zut grunts for Hoom's help|audio=Zut-Hoom-Ewoks1-3.flac|<ref name="Rampage" />}}
[[File:HoomPlay-RotP.png|thumb|left|300px|Hoom plays for his little brother.]]
Hoom was a [[Youngling/Legends|young]] [[Phlog/Legends|Phlog]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. He was the son of the Phlogs [[Zut]] and [[Dobah]] and the older brother of [[Nahkee]].<ref name="CSWE" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{EwoksDate|the appearance of Hoom|[[Rampage of the Phlogs]]}}</ref> Hoom and his family traveled to the area around [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] on Endor, which was home to the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. While their [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were busy, Hoom entertained Nahkee by blowing leaves off a [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and having them rain down, much to his little brother's amusement. Zut then requested Hoom's help with a boulder, so Hoom dutifully ran over. An unsupervised Nahkee then trailed off into the [[Forest/Legends|forest]], where he was [[First Phlog crisis|kidnapped]] by the [[Witch/Legends|witch]] [[Morag]]. The witch then planted evidence to imply that the Ewoks had been behind the crime, trying to get the Phlogs to attack their village.<ref name="Rampage" />

Meanwhile, Dobah realized Nahkee was missing and alerted her husband and son, who rushed over in dismay. Hoom noticed tracks leading away—he and his parents followed them and found Ewok [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] and [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] at the end, so they concluded that the Ewoks must have taken their son, with Morag cackling to herself as she watched. The Phlogs stomped away toward the village, and Zut ran into four [[Wokling/Legends|woklings]]. The young Ewoks ran away into a hollow log, in which they rolled off a cliff and into a [[Water/Legends|river]]. Hoom and Dobah joined Zut, peering over the precipice at the now-swimming Ewoks. They bellowed "Ewoks!" at them but were unable to reach them.<ref name="Rampage" />

The three Phlogs eventually reached more Ewoks, and Dobah accused them of stealing her baby, which the Ewok [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] translated for the rest. He and [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] deduced that Morag must have framed them, but the Phlogs then launched a boulder at the cliff, tearing a chunk of it off and forcing Logray to use [[Magic/Legends|magic]] to save the woklings [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]]. Hoom, Zut, and Dobah continued clamoring at them in their [[Phlog language|language]].<ref name="Rampage" />

===In for a Phlogging===
{{Quote|Yes, Zut, the Ewoks are your friends.|Logray makes peace with the Phlogs|audio=Logray-PhlogFriends-Ewoks1-3.flac|<ref name="Rampage" />}}
[[File:HoomRealization-RotP.png|thumb|right|300px|Hoom (far right) realizes his brother has returned.]]
Hoom and his parents ultimately reached the village proper, whose warriors were geared up for battle. Dobah began by throwing a boulder at the Ewoks, to which they responded with a rock of their own—however, though it hit Zut's chest, he brushed it off as though it was a pebble. Morag looked on excitedly as the Phlogs prepared to trample Bright Tree Village, but just before they started, the woklings [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]], [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], Kintaka, and Latara returned with Nahkee from the [[Dulok Swamp]], where Morag had hidden the baby. The commotion awoke Nahkee, whose bawling distracted his family from decimating the Ewoks. Dobah ran over to pick him up and was followed by Hoom and Zut, who welcomed the baby back.<ref name="Rampage" />

Nahkee told his family that the Ewoks had saved him, and just then, the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] arrived to recover their [[Prisoner/Legends|captive]]. Their [[King/Legends|king]], [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]], began to demand him back, but he trailed off at the sight of the imposing Phlogs. Zut thus realized who had really been holding his son hostage, and he scared them off by stomping his [[Limb/Legends|foot]], much to the mirth of the woklings. He then shook hands with Logray, acknowledging the Ewoks as the Phlogs' friends, and he and his family walked away.<ref name="Rampage" />

==Personality and traits==
Hoom enjoyed making his brother giggle, and he was quick to help his father. He became furious at the kidnapping of Nahkee. Hoom had [[Color/Legends|light brown]] skin and reddish [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and he spoke the Phlog [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="Rampage" />

==Equipment==
Hoom wore a purple [[loincloth]].<ref name="Rampage" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hoom first appeared in "[[Rampage of the Phlogs]]," the third episode of the animated series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks''{{'}}]] [[Ewoks: Season One|first season]],<ref name="Rampage" /> which aired on [[September 21]], [[1985]].<ref name="I27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> He was voiced by [[John Stocker]].<ref name="teoaats">{{BookCite|author=Perlmutter, David|title=The Encyclopedia of American Animated Television Shows|publisher=[[Wikipedia:Rowman & Littlefield|Rowman & Littlefield]]|date=2019-05-04|isbn=978-1538103739|link=https://archive.org/details/the-encyclopedia-of-american-animated-television-shows-perlmutter-david-z-lib.org}}</ref>
